channel.title: NPR Topics: Science
channel.description: The latest health and science news. Updates on medicine, healthy living, nutrition, drugs, diet, and advances in science and technology. Subscribe to the Health & Science podcast.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: 'My body carried me,' Elizabeth Smart says. Now she's celebrating it
item.description: Her abduction at age 14 drew international attention. After her rescue, Smart says she struggled with feeling shame around her body. Bodybuilding has helped her see herself differently.
item.content:encoded:
Her abduction at age 14 drew international attention. After her rescue, Smart says she struggled with feeling shame around her body. Bodybuilding has helped her see herself differently.

(Image credit: Kim Raff for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Windsor Johnston

item.title: California chemical tank has cracked, causing state of emergency, thousands to evacuate
item.description: One California town is in a state of emergency and 50,000 people are under an evacuation order as a malfunctioning chemical tank at an aerospace plant is overheating and could leak or explode.
item.content:encoded:
One California town is in a state of emergency and 50,000 people are under an evacuation order as a malfunctioning chemical tank at an aerospace plant is overheating and could leak or explode.

(Image credit: Apu Gomes)
item.dc:creator: Geoff Brumfiel

item.title: The Enhanced Games are Sunday. Here's what to know about the controversial event
item.description: Dozens of athletes — including former Olympians — will participate in the Las Vegas event while using performance-enhancing drugs.
item.content:encoded:
Dozens of athletes — including former Olympians — will participate in the Las Vegas event while using performance-enhancing drugs.

(Image credit: Ty ONeil)
item.dc:creator: Ava Berger

item.title: SpaceX launches its biggest, most beefed-up Starship yet on a test flight
item.description: The mega rocket made its debut two days after SpaceX CEO Elon Musk announced he's taking the company public. NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman said Starship is now one step closer to the moon.
item.content:encoded:
The mega rocket made its debut two days after SpaceX CEO Elon Musk announced he's taking the company public. NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman said Starship is now one step closer to the moon.

(Image credit: Eric Gay)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: SpaceX to launch Starship rocket Friday after delay
item.description: SpaceX scrubbed the twelfth launch of its newly redesigned Starship moments before liftoff Thursday. The company will try again Friday and it comes at a critical time for SpaceX, which is seeking to go public.
item.content:encoded: SpaceX scrubbed the twelfth launch of its newly redesigned Starship moments before liftoff Thursday. The company will try again Friday and it comes at a critical time for SpaceX, which is seeking to go public.
item.dc:creator: Geoff Brumfiel

item.title: Why a decades-old forest planting practice from Japan is gaining traction in the U.S.
item.description: Communities across the U.S. are turning small plots of land into highly dense forests that grow quickly. Turns out these forests have roots to a decades-old planting method that originated in Japan.
item.content:encoded:
Communities across the U.S. are turning small plots of land into highly dense forests that grow quickly. Turns out these forests have roots to a decades-old planting method that originated in Japan.

(Image credit: Lauren Gallup)
item.dc:creator: Lauren Gallup

item.title: A trillion dollar question: Will SpaceX's Starship launch go well?
item.description: Ahead of a much anticipated IPO, SpaceX is carrying out a critical test of its giant, stainless steel rocket. Investors will be watching closely.
item.content:encoded:
Ahead of a much anticipated IPO, SpaceX is carrying out a critical test of its giant, stainless steel rocket. Investors will be watching closely.

(Image credit: Eric Gay)
item.dc:creator: Geoff Brumfiel

item.title: Researchers say the Trump administration is finding new ways to punish science
item.description: Even with federal grants largely restored, scientists say the Trump administration is still preventing those funds from reaching them. The consequences, they say, are already becoming clear.
item.content:encoded:
Even with federal grants largely restored, scientists say the Trump administration is still preventing those funds from reaching them. The consequences, they say, are already becoming clear.

(Image credit: Jodi Hilton for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Katia Riddle

item.title: COVID is shaping Americans' reaction to Ebola and hantavirus
item.description: Some Americans seem to be extra-alarmed about Ebola and hantavirus in the wake of COVID-19. But public health experts say they don't expect another pandemic this time.
item.content:encoded:
Some Americans seem to be extra-alarmed about Ebola and hantavirus in the wake of COVID-19. But public health experts say they don't expect another pandemic this time.

(Image credit: Drew Angerer)
item.dc:creator: Ava Berger

item.title: A guide to converting your lawn into a wildlife friendly garden
item.description: Turning your grass into a garden isn't as complicated as you think, but it will take time and effort. This step-by-step guide breaks down the process, from killing your lawn to picking plants to grow.
item.content:encoded:
Turning your grass into a garden isn't as complicated as you think, but it will take time and effort. This step-by-step guide breaks down the process, from killing your lawn to picking plants to grow.

(Image credit: Celia Llopis-Jepsen/KCUR)
item.dc:creator: Celia Llopis-Jepsen
